name: Missouri Physical Therapist License
description: Already licensed as a physical therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Missouri - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

provider.name: Missouri Board of Healing Arts — PT

address.description: 3605 Missouri Boulevard, P.O. Box 4, Jefferson City, MO 65102

requiredDocuments.name: Official Transcripts
requiredDocuments.description: Sent directly from your educational institution.

requiredDocuments.name: Exam Score Report
requiredDocuments.description: Official verification of passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination).

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does a Missouri physical therapist license cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: For endorsement applicants, the state licensing fee is $80. Additional third-party costs (fingerprinting, transcripts, or verification) may apply.
Yes, passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination) is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

mainEntity.name: Does Missouri have reciprocity for physical therapists?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Missouri participates in the PT Compact. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Missouri without a separate endorsement application.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my physical therapist license to Missouri?

acceptedAnswer.text: Current board processing time is 4 to 6 Weeks once your file is complete.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in Missouri while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.
